what_it_does: A Node.js build gate that parses a TSX/JS file with TypeScript, checks for syntax errors via ts.transpileModule, then walks the AST to reject non-'react' static imports/exports, dynamic import(), require() calls, and external URL string literals.
observations: Uses ts.transpileModule for fast syntax checking without loading a project or types
Allowed module specifiers are hardcoded to 'react' or paths starting with 'react/'
Detects dynamic import() by checking ts.isCallExpression with expression.kind === ts.SyntaxKind.ImportKeyword
Flags string literals matching /^(https?:)?\/\//i as external URLs
Exits 1 with stderr messages on syntax or policy violations
Reads the target file path from process.argv[2]
standouts: AST-based policy enforcement rather than regex
Very narrow allowlist: only react and react/... subpaths are permitted
Treats any external URL literal as a forbidden exfil/remote-fetch signal
conventions: Process exit codes: 0 for safe, 1 for rejection
Comments explain the security rationale for each rule
concerns: Hardcoded allowlist may reject legitimate same-origin /api imports if they are not named react
Does not inspect variable or concatenated module specifiers for dynamic import or require
URL regex could flag protocol-relative strings used in non-fetch contexts
No source-map or line/column reporting for AST violations
notes: A strict, AST-driven pre-promotion scanner that narrows extension code to React-only imports and no remote URLs.
questions: How is this gate invoked in the build/promotion pipeline?
What happens when a file uses a React subpath that does not start with 'react/'?
Are template literals with substitutions intentionally excluded from the external URL check?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it performs the advertised syntax/transpile check and an AST walk that rejects the listed constructs.
intention_signals: The file tries to enforce a minimal, React-only runtime surface for user-coded extensions by blocking dynamic loading, CommonJS requires, and external network references at build time.
novelty: Combines TypeScript transpile diagnostics with a custom AST denylist to catch spaced require(, dynamic import(), and URL literals that regex-only gates might miss.
contribution: Provides a deterministic, AST-based policy check that can be reused or composed into a build pipeline to reject non-conforming extension code before promotion.
summary_for_embedding: An AST-based build gate for TSX/JS extension files that permits only React imports and rejects dynamic imports, require, and external URL literals.
intention_for_embedding: To block remote-code and exfiltration vectors in self-coded extensions during build promotion.

symbols.does: Tests whether a string starts with an external URL scheme or a protocol-relative URL.
symbols.description: A small predicate that returns true for strings matching /^(https?:)?\/\//i, used to flag string literals that might represent remote fetches or exfiltration endpoints.

symbols.does: Recursively walks the TypeScript AST to collect import/export, dynamic import, require, and external URL violations.
symbols.description: Visits each node in the source file, applying the policy rules: static imports/exports must target 'react' or 'react/...', dynamic import() and require() calls are forbidden, and any string literal that looks like an external URL is recorded as a violation.
